response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software-engineering experience across multiple domains, this role requires specialized expertise in embedded security firmware, networking protocols, and security technologies that are entirely absent from the resume. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is a hard requirement that the candidate does not possess. The candidate's 15-year career has focused on web, mobile, and application development; the last C/C++ embedded work was in 2014-2015. The combination of missing Japanese language skills, zero security engineering background, outdated embedded systems experience, and no demonstrated knowledge of encryption, authentication systems, or network security protocols creates a fundamental mismatch.
strengths: Extensive software development background (15+ years) across diverse technical domains demonstrates broad engineering fundamentals and adaptability
Some C/C++ and Linux systems experience from National Instruments and system-level projects provides a foundation for embedded work
Strong DevOps and infrastructure skills (CI/CD, Docker, GitLab administration) show capability to manage complex technical systems
concerns: No demonstrated security expertise—resume contains zero mention of encryption, authentication systems, attack detection, or any security-focused work required for the role
Native-level Japanese proficiency is a stated hard requirement; resume lists English, Romanian, and German only—this is disqualifying
Last embedded systems and C/C++ work ended in 2014-2015 (10+ years ago); recent career focused on high-level web/application development with TypeScript and Node.js, not embedded firmware

key_factors.factor: No security engineering background or encryption/authentication expertise
Outdated embedded systems experience and C/C++ skills (10-year gap)
